title: Object Oriented Programming in C# Workshop
headline: Finally Understand Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism and Abstraction - and Actually Use Them
subheadline: A live, hands-on workshop where you'll master the four pillars of OOP in C# - from classes and constructors to interfaces, polymorphism and real-world design decisions.
painPoints: You can write C# that compiles, but your classes are just bags of data with no real structure
Inheritance, interfaces, abstract classes - you've read about them but can't decide when to use which
Your code works today but becomes a nightmare to extend or maintain next month
You see UML diagrams and design patterns in documentation but can't translate them into working C# code
transformation: Design classes that are clean, intentional and easy to extend - not accidental blobs of logic
Choose confidently between inheritance, interfaces and abstract classes for any real scenario
Apply all four OOP pillars - encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism and abstraction - with clarity
Read a UML diagram and translate it into well-structured C# code without hesitation
Write code that other developers can understand, maintain and build upon
whatYouWillDo: Build classes from scratch - constructors, access modifiers, properties, static members - and understand exactly why each exists
Implement inheritance hierarchies, method overriding and sealed classes on real-world scenarios
Design and implement interfaces, including advanced patterns like multiple interface implementation
Compare abstract classes vs interfaces through practical use cases so you always know which to reach for
Explore both static and dynamic polymorphism with hands-on examples that make the difference click
Work through value types vs reference types, troubleshooting common class-related bugs and error handling
Apply naming conventions, code structure best practices
Complete practical exercises with live corrections - so you walk away having actually built something, not just watched
prerequisites: You should be comfortable with basic C# syntax - variables, methods, loops and conditionals. You don't need prior OOP experience - that's exactly what we'll build together.
whyDifferent: Every concept is coded live in C# - not explained on slides you'll forget by Monday
Hands-on exercises after each topic with live corrections so you understand exactly where your thinking went wrong
The sandbox environment stays available after the workshop - keep practising and experimenting on your own
Recordings stay accessible for a period so you can revisit any explanation at your own pace
Cheatsheets provided - a practical OOP reference you'll keep reaching for long after the last session
Small batch size so you get real attention, real answers and real feedback - not a seat in a crowd
Designed around real developer problems - you'll solve the exact challenges that trip people up in production codebases
A badge to showcase your new skills on LinkedIn and get noticed by employers

instructor.name: Mehfooz Kausmally
instructor.description: A seasoned software professional from Mauritius with over a decade of experience in the IT industry. After a successful career as a developer in multinational companies working extensively with C#, .NET and complex backend systems, Mehfooz transitioned into full-time professional coaching. He has already guided over 270 engineers across different technologies and levels - bringing real-world expertise and a practical, hands-on approach into every session.
